chat.author: Alex Huang Feng

chat.author: Benoît Claise
chat.text: draft-kbf-onsen-problem-statement authors, please insert your github location in the document datatracker "additional resources" field

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: Maybe another question for this problem statement is who is willing to work on this?  There are a lot of possible abstractions, but those that have committed workers should be prioritized.  When we were socializing charter feedback, we heard from some operators and vendors that there were specific service/network abstractions they were willing to work on.

chat.author: Robert Wilton
chat.text: Sorry, I seemed to have logged out of the session.  I would regard problem 2 as being definitely in scope for Onsen.

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: I think the Problem 2 is a little more clear than Problem 1, but it may be just me.

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: wasnt Mapping between service to network was considered out of scope in onsen;

chat.author: Robert Wilton
chat.text: Mahesh, I agree, it wasn't that clear to me exactly what problem 1 really meant.

chat.text: misalignment is surely in scope but not mapping!

chat.text: I would say what we need is consistency…

chat.text: +1 to consistency.

chat.author: Benoît Claise
chat.text: one problem statement 3, we would all like to have a standard/opensource orchestrator controller mapping, basically having a free lunch but I am afraid we won't see this happening

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text:
4.5.2. Divergence Between YANG Models and Generated APIs
APIs generated from similar YANG models often differ in service semantics, complicating integration across systems, vendors, and deployment environments.
4.6

chat.author: Robert Wilton
chat.text: Problem 5 looks to clearly be in the ONSEN charter, but I would suggest that the work is deferred behind some of the other work.

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: @Rob, what other work do you see that needs to be done before this work is picked up?

chat.author: Michael Mackey
chat.text: @Dhruv this is the key point but most people treat yang like an api model and L3xM are awful apis

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Did we send out liasons like we planned in the charter?

chat.author: Robert Wilton
chat.text: ONSEN has lots of other work to align the SM and NM models.  I was jus thinking that it would make sense to solve those (or mostly solve those) and then start looking at the relationship between the SM models and TM640/641.  This approach would also seem to align with the milestones.

chat.author: Oscar de Dios
chat.text: +1 step by step

chat.text: I have the same question as Dhruv, it is making current modules into reusable groupings or is there also other things in the scope?

chat.text: service model gets polluted by CE-PE technology layer information

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: I was a bit surprised ietf-vpn-common didn't come up here, but Oscar and Italo touched on it.  I would think if there is a need for something higher-level, that could be created, and then this vpn-common could be looked to be updated to work with that.

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: If there's a big impact on YANG refactoring hopefully VELOCE will come in handy

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: @Maesh, you would be surprised how much enterprise customer actual "care" about these things and headlock ISP with RFP/SLAs and detailed reports

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: *surprised

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: @Don, are you suggesting that if an operator offers their services say over SR-MPLS and it meets the SLA of the customer, that the customer would not buy the service unless it was over SRv6?

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: s/Don/Dan/

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: pretty much yes, although your examples with segment routing are extreme and not realist as these are relatively new - but in that line, "since you are using these examples"  - ATM vs MPLS network yes.

chat.author: James Cumming
Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: Interesting! Because a requirement like that means that the operator  would need a forklift every time such a ask comes from the customer.

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: these things comes via RFP (same as vendor - operator) another easy one would be instead of saying "SRv6" it could be  "IPv6 end-to-end"

chat.author: Muthumayan Madhayyan
chat.text:
For the dynamic network use case, how many nodes does a typical VPN have?
Is that in 10s, 100s .. etc?

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: Are these extensions going in via augmentations or bis?

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: draft-ietf-teas-ietf-network-slice-nbi-yang-26 is in RFC editor queue

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: augments

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Our milestones has - Aug 2026   Send LSes to TMF, 3GPP, BBF, GSMA, Linux Foundation, TIP, and EANTC about ONSEN

chat.author: Fengchao FU
chat.text: For the dynamic network use case，VPN with several nodes are the typical scenario

chat.author: Fengchao FU
chat.text: Our main scenario is data transfer, so point-to-point topology is used most

chat.author: Fengchao FU
chat.text: We intend to implement these new features via augment as much as possible

chat.author: Daniel Bernier
chat.text: BBF as part of their "Intent Automation Tiger Team work" did a lot of effort around service/resource/intent which is worth looking at (major thanks to NBN) for this.

chat.author: Daniel Bernier
chat.text: one question which i have is around the concept of "atomic" or "composite" modeling

chat.author: Daniel Bernier
chat.text: i.e. one NM could potentially reference sub NMs would it be part of YANG 2.0 ?

chat.author: Cancan Huang
chat.text: the service interconnection requirements in tele-cloud should be full-dimensional, I propose to add two dimensions to the abstraction: resource cost and compute-electricity coordination.

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: would we consider this in scope of charter?

chat.author: Cancan Huang
chat.text: cost and revenue can influence the network solution selection. One dedicated  service model may be mapped to the various network model based on the cost. So I think it need to be in the scope
